title: Termination of contract without notice by employer
content:
(1)	
An employer may terminate a contract of employment 
without notice or payment in lieu—    (Amended 51 of 2000 
s. 2)
	
(a)	
if an employee, in relation to his employment—
	
(i)	
wilfully disobeys a lawful and reasonable order;
	
(ii)	
misconducts 
himself, 
such 
conduct 
being 
inconsistent with the due and faithful discharge of 
his duties;
	
(iii)	
is guilty of fraud or dishonesty; or
	
(iv)	
is habitually neglectful in his duties; or
	
(b)	
on any other ground on which he would be entitled to 
terminate the contract without notice at common law.
	
(2)	
The fact that an employee takes part in a strike does not 
entitle his employer to terminate under subsection (1) the 
employee’s contract of employment. (Added 51 of 2000 s. 2)

metadata.source_file: Cap 57 Consolidated version for the Whole Chapter (24-08-2025) (English).pdf
